weight.read: Fourteen weigh-ins over fourteen days is good coverage, so the trend is readable. The 7-day average sits at 82.4 kg and the regression slope is -0.2 kg/week — a genuine, small loss, not noise. The problem is pace: reaching 78 kg by July 30 requires ~0.47 kg/week over the 10.3 weeks remaining, roughly 2.4× your current rate. At -0.2 kg/week you arrive at 78 kg around mid-October, not July 30.
actions: Log everything you eat for three consecutive days this week — not to judge the food, but to locate a specific 200–250 kcal/day reduction (one less high-calorie drink, a smaller portion at one meal); that exact cut is what gets you from -0.2 to ~0.47 kg/week without going below a healthy deficit.

If the food log reveals no clean 200–250 kcal lever without significant disruption, move the target date to mid-October rather than forcing a larger deficit — the current rate is real progress and mid-October is an honest, healthy timeline.
